text: On the FIFA World Cup: 1930 – 2014, 84 years of history Alexandre JANOT ONERA – DCSD – IDCO Toulouse – 26 January 2017
FIFA World Cup 1930 The first FIFA World Cup! The history begins in Uruguay! 2

FIFA World Cup 1930 Tournament details : •Host country: Uruguay (1 st time); •Dates: 13–30 July (18 days); •Teams: 13; •Venue(s): 3 (in 1 host city – Montevideo). Final positions : •Champions: Uruguay (1 st title); Key points Estadio Centenario 3Champions: Uruguay (1 title); •Runners-up: Argentina; •Third place: USA; •Fourth place: Yugoslavia. Tournament statistics : •Matches played: 18; •Goals scored: 70 (3.89 per match); •Attendance: 590,549 (32,808 per match). Uruguay's fourth goal was scored by striker Héctor C astro.

FIFA World Cup 1930 Highlights Host selection : •Uruguay would be celebrating the centenary of its f irst constitution; •Uruguay had successfully retained their football ti tle at the 1928 Summer Olympics; •Uruguay accepted to cover the expanses. Notes : •The first World Cup was the only one without qualifi cation and 2 months before the start of the tournament, no team from Europe had official ly entered; •FIFA president Jules Rimet intervened, and four European teams made the trip b y sea: 4•FIFA president Jules Rimet intervened, and four European teams made the trip b y sea: Belgium, France, Romania, and Yugoslavia; •Lucien Laurent of France scored the first goal in Wor ld Cup history. Results : •Argentina, Uruguay, the United States and Yugoslavi a each won their respective groups to qualify for the semi-finals; •Argentina defeated USA by 6-1 while Uruguay won ove r Yugoslavia by 6-1; •Uruguay won its first star by defeating Argentina 4 -2.
FIFA World Cup 1934 The first political FIFA WC! Dark -blue -shirt (black -shirt?) Italy wins its 1st star! 5wins its 1 star!

FIFA World Cup 1934 Tournament details : •Host country: Italy (1 st time); •Dates: 27 May – 10 June (15 days); •Teams: 16 (from 4 confederations); •Venue(s): 8 (in 8 host cities). Final positions : •Champions: Italy (1 st title); Key points Stadio Nazionale PNF 6Champions: Italy (1 title); •Runners-up: Czechoslovakia; •Third place: Germany; •Fourth place: Austria. Tournament statistics : •Matches played: 17; •Goals scored: 70 (4.12 per match); •Attendance: 363,000 (21,353 per match).Stadio Nazionale PNF Italian team line up saluting Benito Mussolini

FIFA World Cup 1934 Highlights Host selection : •Italian bid was chosen in preference to one from Sw eden. Notes : •Only one in which the reigning champions did not pa rticipate; •First and only time the host nation was not granted automatic qualification; •Egypt is the first African team to participate; •This WC high-profile instance of a sporting event b eing used for overt political gain; •Mussolini keen to use the tournament as a means of promoting fascism. 7•Mussolini keen to use the tournament as a means of promoting fascism. Results : •Italy enters its 1 st final by eliminating USA (7-1), Spain (1-1 and 1-0 after a rematch), Austria which was one of the best European team at this time (1-0); •Czechoslovakia enters its 1 st final by dispatching Romania (2-1), Switzerland (3- 2) and Germany (3-1); •Italy wins its first crown by beating Czechoslovaki a by 2-1 aet; •The match was really stressing and of good quality; •Despite the fact Italy won at home before Mussolini , it appears that he didn’t interfere to ‘help’ Itlay.
FIFA World Cup 1938 The last FIFA WC before WWII! La seconda coppa per l’Italia di Benito 8Benito

FIFA World Cup 1938 Key points Tournament details : •Host country: France (1 st time); •Dates: 4 June – 19 June (16 days); •Teams: 15 (from 4 confederations); •Venue(s): 10 (in 10 host cities). Final positions : •Champions: Italy (2 nd title); Pozzo the mastermind as Italy retain their crown 9Champions: Italy (2 title); •Runners-up: Hungary; •Third place: Brazil; •Fourth place: Sweden. Tournament statistics : •Matches played: 18; •Goals scored 84 (4.67 per match); •Attendance: 375,700 (20,872 per match). Reims Vélodrome Municipal

FIFA World Cup 1938 Highlights Host selection : •France defeated Argentina and Germany in the first round of voting. Notes : •Austria subsequently withdrew from the tournament b ecause of Anschluss; •First appearances: Cuba, Dutch East Indies (now Ind onesia), Poland and Norway; •A unified Germany team would not appear again until 19 94. Results : •Italy enters its 1 st final by eliminating Norway (2 -1), France (3 -1) and Brazil (2 -1) while 10 •Italy enters its 1 st final by eliminating Norway (2 -1), France (3 -1) and Brazil (2 -1) while Brazilian coach decided to ‘rest’ one of the best p layers Leônidas for the final (strange and stupid strategy); Brazilians over confident? No, of course ☺; •Hungary enters its first final by destroying Dutch East Indies (6-0), winning over Switzerland by 2-0 and hammering Sweden 5-1; •Italy wins its second crown by beating Hungary by 4 -2; •Italy became the first team to successfully defend the title.
FIFA World Cup 1950 Brazil crucified by Ghiggia ! Uruguay 2, Brazil 0! 11

Tournament details : •Host country: Brazil (1 st time); •Dates: 16 June – 4 July (19 days); •Teams: 16 (from 4 confederations); •Venue(s): 6 (in 6 host cities). Final positions : •Champions: Uruguay (2 nd title); FIFA World Cup 1950 Key points 12 Champions: Uruguay (2 title); •Runners-up: Brazil; •Third place: Sweden; •Fourth place: Spain. Tournament statistics : •Matches played: 22; •Goals scored: 88 (4,0 per match); •Attendance: 1,045,246 (47,511 per match). The Maracana stadium

Highlights Host selection : •Brazil was awarded the tournament unopposed. Notes : •A lot of withdrawals (impossible to list them, see Wikipedia); •Italian team decimated because of the Superga aircra sh disaster (travelled by boat rather than by plane). Results : •The final round involved Brazil, Uruguay, Spain and Sweden; FIFA World Cup 1950 13 •The final round involved Brazil, Uruguay, Spain and Sweden; •Brazil defeated Sweden by 7-1 and Spain by 6-1 whil e Uruguay drawed against Spain 2-2 and won over Sweden by 3-2; •Big surprise when Uruguay won over Brazil before it s audience by 2-1; •Friaça oppened the score in the 47th minute but Schia ffino equalized in the 66th minute and Ghiggia crucified Brazil and its supporters in the 79th minute! •Uruguay became the second team to win a second crow n after Italy.
FIFA World Cup 1954 A Taste of Swiss Chocolate! Das Wunder von Bern! 14

Tournament details : •Host country: Switzerland (1 st time); •Dates: 16 June – 4 July (19 days); •Teams: 16 (from 4 confederations); •Venue(s): 6 (in 6 host cities). Final positions : •Champions: West Germany (1 st title); FIFA World Cup 1954 Key points 15 Champions: West Germany (1 title); •Runners-up: Hungary; •Third place: Austria; •Fourth place: Uruguay. Tournament statistics : •Matches played: 26; •Goals scored: 140 (5.38 per match); •Attendance: 768,607 (29,562 per match). Morlock equalizing for West Germany

Highlights Host selection : •Switzerland was awarded the tournament unopposed. First times : •First appearances: Scotland, Turkey and South Korea . Notes : •Spain was eliminated by Turkey; •All-time records were set or equaled at this tourna ment, and have not been surpassed (see Wikipedia ☺). FIFA World Cup 1954 16 (see Wikipedia ☺). Results : •West Germany enter its 1 st final by dispatching Yugoslavia (2-0) and defeating Austria (6- 1); •Hungary enters its 2nd and last final by beating Brazil (4-2) in a very vi olent match called ‘Battle of Berne ’ and Uruguay (4-2, aet); •West Germany won its first crown over Hungary by 3- 2 whereas it was hammered 8-3 by this same Hungarian team in the group matches (iron ic isn’t it?? ☺but Puskas was injured).
FIFA World Cup 1958 FIFA WC hosted in Sweden! After the 1950 disillusion, the first Brazilian samba! 17 Brazilian samba!

Tournament details : •Host country: Sweden (1 st time); •Dates: 8–29 June (22 days); •Teams: 16 (from 3 confederations); •Venue(s): 12 (in 12 host cities). Final positions : •Champions: Brazil (1 st title); FIFA World Cup 1958 Key points Brazilian team celebrating its first crown 18 Champions: Brazil (1 title); •Runners-up: Sweden; •Third place: France; •Fourth place: West Germany. Tournament statistics : •Matches played: 35; •Goals scored: 126 (3.6 per match); •Attendance: 819,810 (23,423 per match). France's Just Fontaine celebrates netting 13 goals at the 1958 tournament - but was it all real?Brazilian team celebrating its first crown

Highlights Host selection : •4 bidding nations involved: Argentina, Chile, Mexic o, and Sweden; •Swedish delegates lobbied other countries at the FI FA Congress held in Rio de Janeiro in 1950. First times : •First appearance: USSR; •Qualification of all the United Kingdom's Home Natio ns: England, Scotland, Wales, and Northern Ireland. FIFA World Cup 1958 19 Northern Ireland. Results : •Brazil enters its final by defeating Wales (1-0) an d France (5-2); •Sweden enters its final by defeating USSR (2-0) an d West Germany (3-1); •France won its 3 rd place by beating West Germany 5-2; •Brazil defeated Sweden by 5-2 and became the 1 st (and the last!) south-American team to win its crown in Europe.
FIFA World Cup 1962 A Culminating Violent Football! La segunda brasileira samba! 20

Tournament details : •Host country: Chile (1 st time); •Dates: May 30 – June 17 (19 days); •Teams: 16 (from 3 confederations); •Venue(s): 4 (in 4 host cities). Final positions : •Champions: Brazil (2 nd title); FIFA World Cup 1962 Key points Italy's Giorgio Ferrini is removed from the pitch by policemen! 21 Champions: Brazil (2 title); •Runners-up: Czechoslovakia; •Third place: Chile; •Fourth place: Yugoslavia. Tournament statistics : •Matches played: 32; •Goals scored: 89 (2.78 per match); •Attendance: 893,172 (27,912 per match). Zito celebrating his goal

Highlights Host selection : •2 bidding nations involved: Chile and Argentina; •Chile won 32 votes to Argentina's 11. First times : •Use of goal average to separate teams with the same amount of points. Notes : •Various FA worry about the country's ability to org anize the tournament in comparison to Argentina's superior sports infrastructure and prestige; FIFA World Cup 1962 22 to Argentina's superior sports infrastructure and prestige; •Chile earthquakeed before the tournament (Valdvia 196 0); •Violent atmosphere culminated in the match between Chile and Italy (2–0) known as the ‘ Battle of Santiago ’ (Ken Aston (referee) sent two Italians off and po lice had to intervene). Results : •Brazil enters its final by defeating England (3-1) and Chile (4-2); •Czechoslovakia enters its final by defeating Hungar y (1-0) and Yugoslavia (3-1); •Brazil re-defeated Czechoslovakia by 3-1 and became the 2 nd team to succeed to retain its title.
FIFA World Cup 1966 God saves the Football! At last, England wins its (controversial? ☺) 1st crown! 23 (controversial? ☺) 1 crown!

Tournament details : •Host country: England (1 st time); •Dates: 11–30 July (20 days); •Teams: 16 (from 4 confederations); •Venue(s): 8 (in 7 host cities). Final positions : •Champions: England (1 st title); FIFA World Cup 1966 Key points 24 Champions: England (1 title); •Runners-up: West Germany; •Third place: Portugal; •Fourth place: USSR. Tournament statistics : •Matches played: 32; •Goals scored: 89 (2.78 per match); •Attendance: 1,563,135 (48,848 per match). The German referee struggling in explaining why Rat tin is sent off

Highlights Host selection : •3 bidding nations involved: England, West Germany a nd Spain. First times : •First participation: Portugal and North Korea. Notes : •Sixteen African nations boycotted the tournament in protest at a 1964 FIFA ruling (the 3 2 nd -round winners from the African zone required to en ter a play-off round against the winners of the Asian zone in order to win a place a t the finals ). FIFA World Cup 1966 25 winners of the Asian zone in order to win a place a t the finals ). Results : •Biggest surprise: Italy beaten by North Korea 0-1 a nd eliminated; •Controversial victory of West Germany over Uruguay by 4-0 (2 Uruguayans sent off); •Portugal eliminated North Korea by 5-3 after being headed 0-3 (many thanks Eusebio); •Controversial victory of England over Argentina by 1-0 (1 Argentinian sent off); •Controversial victory of England over West Germany (2-2, 4-2 aet): lot of mistakes from the referee and Hurst’s shot hit the crossbar, boun ced down onto the goal line, and was awarded as a goal; •Need to improve the arbitration and find a clear wa y to warn and expel a player.
FIFA World Cup 1970 Shiny Football 4, Catenaccio 1! La terceira muito bonita e ultima samba por rei Pele! 26 samba por rei Pele!

Tournament details : •Host country: Mexico (1 st time); •Dates: 31 May – 21 June 1970 (22 days); •Teams: 16 (from 5 confederations); •Venue(s): 5 (in 5 host cities). Final positions : •Champions: Brazil (3 rd title); FIFA World Cup 1970 Key points 27 Champions: Brazil (3 title); •Runners-up: Italy; •Third place: West Germany; •Fourth place: Uruguay. Tournament statistics : •Matches played: 32; •Goals scored: 95 (2.97 per match); •Attendance: 1,604,065 (50,127 per match). Estadio Azteca

Highlights Host selection : •6 bidding nations involved: Argentina, Australia, C olombia, Japan, Mexico and Peru; •Mexico won over Argentina. First times : •First participation: El Salvador, Israel, and Morocco ; •First WC hosted in North America, and staged outsid e South America and Europe; •Idea of language-neutral colored cards originated w ith English referee Ken Aston (got the idea while sitting in his car at a traffic ligh t); FIFA World Cup 1970 28 the idea while sitting in his car at a traffic ligh t); •The first major use of the cards (no players sent o ff in that tournament); •Evgeny Lovchev became the first player to be booked on a WC match; •Games were broadcast live around the world and for t he first time in color. Notes : •A war erupted between Honduras and San Salvador bec ause of a play-off match that acted as a catalyst (see Football War); when I say politics and football are linked ☺; •North Korea, quarter-finalists at the previous tour nament, disqualified during the process after refusing to play in Israel for politi cal reasons.

Highlights Results : •West Germany defeated England (4-2 aet) while Brazi l eliminated quietly Peru (4-2), Uruguay won over USSR and Italy eliminated Mexico ( 4-1); •The semi final between Italy and West Germany is co nsidered as one of the most dramatic and impactful semi finals (Game of Century ): Italy won over West Germany 4-3 aet;Italy led for the majority of the match, after Rober to Boninsegna scored in the 8th minute; FIFA World Cup 1970 29 8th minute; Franz Beckenbauer dislocated his shoulder, but staye d on the field carrying his dislocated arm in a sling (already used their two p ermitted substitutions); Karl-Heinz Schnellinger equalized for West Germany d uring injury time; Gerd Müller put Germany ahead in the 94th minute; Tarcisio Burgnich tied it back up four minutes later and Luigi Riva put the Italians back in front; Gerd Müller scored again for West Germany to tie up t he score at 3–3; As television cameras were still replaying Müller's goal, Italy's Gianni Rivera scored the game-winning goal in the 111th minute;

Highlights FIFA World Cup 1970 Results : •In the final, Brazil won over Italy by 4-1: Brazil opened the scoring with Pelé in the 18th minu te; Roberto Boninsegna equalized for Italy after a serie s of blunders in the Brazilian defense; Gérson restored the Brazilians' lead in the 66 th minute; Further goals from Jairzinho and Carlos Alberto rewarded Brazil's attacking play 30 Further goals from Jairzinho and Carlos Alberto rewarded Brazil's attacking play and secured a 4–1 victory; •Brazil won its third crown within 12 years, a recor d.
FIFA World Cup 1974 Locals – Total football: 1 -0! Kaiser Beckenbauer gewinnt gegen den fliegenden Holländer Cruijf ! 31 den fliegenden Holländer Cruijf !

Tournament details : •Host country: West Germany (1 st time); •Dates: 13 June – 7 July (25 days); •Teams: 16 (from 5 confederations); •Venue(s): 9 (in 9 host cities). Final positions : •Champions: West Germany (2 nd title); FIFA World Cup 1974 Key points 32 Champions: West Germany (2 title); •Runners-up: Netherlands; •Third place: Poland; •Fourth place: Brazil. Tournament statistics : •Matches played: 38; •Goals scored: 97 (2.55 per match); •Attendance: 1,865,762 (49,099 per match). Cruijf and Beckenbauer shaking hands before the final

Highlights Host selection : •West Germany agreed a deal with Spain (Spain suppor ts West Germany and West Germany allows Spain to bid for the 1982 World Cup unopposed). First times : •First participation: Australia, East Germany, Haiti a nd Zaire (first sub-Saharan team); •The Jules Rimet Trophy had been won for the third tim e by Brazil in 1970 and awarded permanently to the Brazilians; •Carlos Caszely of Chile became the first player to be sent off with a red card in a World FIFA World Cup 1974 33 •Carlos Caszely of Chile became the first player to be sent off with a red card in a World Cup match. Notes : •The tournament held mostly in bad weather, stadia h ad few protected places; Results : •Yugoslavia won over Zaire by 9-0, Poland won over H aiti by 7-0 (that hurts); •Poland made sensation by defeating Argentina (3–2), Haiti (7–0), then Italy (2–1), respectively; •Italians knocked out of the WC Argentina sneaking t o the 2 nd group round on goal difference;

Highlights Results : •Scotland edged out of the tournament on goal differ ence (could only manage a 2–0 margin against Zaire);•‘Ajax’ dutch team defeated Uruguay (2-0), Bulgaria ( 4-1) and drawed against Sweden (0- 0); •West Germany knocked out Chile (1-0), Australia (3- 0) and East Germany (1-0); •Netherlands defeated Argentina (4-0), East Germany (2-0) and Brazil (2-0); • West Germany defeated Yugoslavia (2 -0), Sweden (4 -2) and Poland (1 -0); FIFA World Cup 1974 34 West Germany defeated Yugoslavia (2 -0), Sweden (4 -2) and Poland (1 -0); •The Total Football team defeated in its first final by the ‘locals’ 1-2 because of Gerd Müller!
FIFA World Cup 1978 Locals – Total football: 2 -0! The Kempes ’ tango (or Videla’s title?)! 35 title?)!

Tournament details : •Host country: Argentina (1 st time); •Dates: 1–25 June (25 days); •Teams: 16 (from 5 confederations); •Venue(s): 6 (in 5 host cities). Final positions : •Champions: Argentina (1 st title); FIFA World Cup 1978 Key points 36 Champions: Argentina (1 title); •Runners-up: Netherlands; •Third place: Brazil; •Fourth place: Italy. Tournament statistics : •Matches played: 38; •Goals scored: 102 (2.68 per match); •Attendance: 1,545,791 (40,679 per match). Kempes lifting the crown and celebrating his goal

Highlights Host selection : •Argentina’s bid unopposed after Mexico’s withdrawal . First times : •First participation: Iran and Tunisia. Notes : •Logo based on Juan Perón's signature gesture and milit ary leadership tried to change that, without success; •Because of the political turmoil, some countries, m ost notably the Netherlands, FIFA World Cup 1978 37 •Because of the political turmoil, some countries, m ost notably the Netherlands, considered publicly whether they should participate in the event. Despite this, all teams eventually took part without restrictions. Results : •Argentina needed to win by a margin of four goals t o proceed to the final and did so by defeating Peru by 6–0 (is it not surprising ☺, political aspects????); •Argentina defeated Netherlands 3-1 aet (thanks to Ke mpes, 2 goals) and won its first crown.
FIFA World Cup 1982 Rossi made his impact! Terza stella per la Squadra Azzurra ! 38

Tournament details : •Host country: Spain (1 st time); •Dates: 13 June – 11 July (29 days); •Teams: 24 (from 6 confederations); •Venue(s): 17 (in 14 host cities). Final positions : •Champions: Italy (3 dtitle); FIFA World Cup 1982 Key points Schumacher’s assault on Battiston 39 Champions: Italy (3 title); •Runners-up: Germany; •Third place: Poland; •Fourth place: France. Tournament statistics : •Matches played: 52; •Goals scored: 146 (2.81 per match); •Attendance: 2,109,723 (40,572 per match). Tardelli and Cabrini celebrating the third star

Highlights Host selection : •Spain’s bid unopposed after an agreement with West Germany. First times : •First participation: Algeria, Cameroon, Honduras, Kuwait and New Zealand; •The first semi-final involving a penalty shoot-out; •The 1982 competition used a unique format. Notes : •FIFA introduced a revised qualification system at s ubsequent World Cups in which the FIFA World Cup 1982 40 •FIFA introduced a revised qualification system at s ubsequent World Cups in which the final two games in each group were played simultane ously. Results : •Hungary defeated El Salvador 10–1, equaling the lar gest margin of victory ever recorded in the finals; •As usual, Italy began with 3 straight draws ☺ •The infamous Disgrace of Gijon: collusion between W est Germany and Austria to eliminate Algeria (that said, if Algeria had won ov er Chile 3-0 (score in the half-time) instead of 3-2, Algeria would have been qualified);

Highlights Results : •Bryan Robson's goal against France after only 27 se conds; •Sheikh Fahad Al-Ahmed Al-Jaber Al-Sabah, brother of t he Kuwaiti Emir and president of the Kuwaiti Football Association, rushed onto the p itch to remonstrate with the referee. Stupar countermanded his initial decision and disall owed the goal to the fury of the French; •The match between Brazil and Italy pitted Brazil's at tack against Italy's defense, with the majority of the game played around the Italian area ; a poor clearance from an Italian FIFA World Cup 1982 41 majority of the game played around the Italian area ; a poor clearance from an Italian corner kick went back to the Brazilian six-yard lin e where Rossi and Francesco Graziani were waiting. Rossi connecting to get a hat trick and sending Italy into the lead for good; •West Germany – France: Schumacher's action has been described as "one of history's most shocking fouls“; Battiston, still unconscious and with a broken jaw, was carried off the field on a stretcher; final score 3-3 after Fra nce headed 3-1 (!)… and France was defeated in penalty shoot-out ; •Nonetheless, Italy koes West Germany and won its 3d crown.
FIFA World Cup 1986 Maradona 2, Thatcher 1! Primera corona por El Pibe de Oro! 42

Tournament details : •Host country: Mexico (2 nd time); •Dates: 31 May – 29 June (30 days); •Teams: 24 (from 5 confederations); •Venue(s): 12 (in 9 host cities). Final positions : •Champions: Argentina (2 nd title); FIFA World Cup 1986 Key points 43 Champions: Argentina (2 title); •Runners-up: Germany; •Third place: France; •Fourth place: Belgium. Tournament statistics : •Matches played: 52; •Goals scored: 132 (2.54 per match); •Attendance: 2,394,031 (46,039 per match). The well known hand of God

Highlights Host selection : •Colombia originally chosen as hosts by FIFA but…; •… could not afford to host the World Cup under the terms that FIFA demanded because of economic concerns;•Mexico won over Canada and the United States of Ame rica. First times : •First participation: Canada, Denmark and Iraq. ; •Mexico the 1st country to host the FIFA WC twice .FIFA World Cup 1986 44 •Mexico the 1st country to host the FIFA WC twice . Notes : •Official mascot was Pique, a jalapeño pepper, chara cteristic of Mexican cuisine, but…; •… caused a degree of controversy within Mexico for its ethnic stereotypes; •Argentina – England had a particular flavor because of the Falklands war (political aspects of football???). Results : •During the group stage, nothing special apart from the defeat of West Germany again Denmark 0-2 and Morocco toped its group; •Then began the craziness of football ☺

Results : •Argentina enters its final by defeating Uruguay (1- 0), England (2-1) and Belgium (2-0); •Argentina – England a crazy game: Maradona scored two times in a different way; first goal scored illegally (Hand of God) whereas the sec ond one considered as the Goal of Century; he dribbled half the length of the field p ast five English players before scoring; •In Argentina, the game seen as revenge for the Falkl ands War (politics??? ☺); •What about France – Brazil?; Careca scores after 18 mi nutes and Platini scored his 41st goal in the 40th minute ; no more goals… penalty shoot -out and Luis Fernandez scored Highlights FIFA World Cup 1986 45 goal in the 40th minute ; no more goals… penalty shoot -out and Luis Fernandez scored the ultimate penalty; •West Germany enters its final by defeating Morocco 1-0, Mexico (0-0, 4-1 penalty shoot-out) and France (once again, 2-0); •Argentina won its second crown over West Germany by 3-2 thanks to… Burruchaga ☺
FIFA World Cup 1990 Welcome back catenaccio ! Der dritte Stern (aber nicht der schönste )! 46 schönste )!

FIFA World Cup 1990 Key points Tournament details : •Host country: Italy (2 nd time); •Dates: 8 June – 8 July (31 days); •Teams: 24 (from 5 confederations); •Venue(s): 12 (in 12 host cities). Final positions : •Champions: Germany (3 rd title); 47 Champions: Germany (3 title); •Runners-up: Argentina; •Third place: Italy; •Fourth place: England. Tournament statistics : •Matches played: 52; •Goals scored: 115 (2.21 per match); •Attendance: 2,516,215 (48,389 per match). Hard times for the once-stared Maradona!

Highlights Host selection : •Two bidding nations involved: Italy and USSR; •Italy won over USSR by 11 votes to 5. First times : •The two semi-finals decided in penalty shootouts; •First participation: Costa Rica, Ireland and the Un ited Arab Emirates; •16 red cards, including the first ever dismissal in a final; •Introduction of the pre -match Fair Play Flag; FIFA World Cup 1990 48 •Introduction of the pre -match Fair Play Flag; •The first time a team from UEFA won the final again st a non-European team. Notes : •Negative tactics led to the introduction of the bac k-pass rule in 1992, and three points for a win instead of two at future World Cups; •1990 World Cup one of the most watched events in te levision history (estimated 26.69 billion non-unique viewers over the course of the tournament); •Because of the semi-finals, idea of the golden goal ; •The England national team forced to play all their matches in Cagliari (because of hooligans).

Results : •Cameroon defeated reigning champions Argentina, 1-0 , despite ending the match with only nine men (!);•Argentina enters its 4 th final after having defeated Brazil (1-0), Yugoslavi a (0-0, 3-2 penalty shoot-out) and Italy (1-1, 4-3 penalty shoo t-out); •West Germany enters its 5 th final after having defeated Netherlands (2-1), Czechoslovakia (1-0) and England (1-1, 4-3 penalty shoot-out); • West Germany wins its 3d crown by defeating Argenti na 1 -0 after a controversial Highlights FIFA World Cup 1990 49 West Germany wins its 3d crown by defeating Argenti na 1 -0 after a controversial penalty scored by Brehme who said there was no foul;•Argentina were the first team to fail to score in a World Cup Final; •Argentina reduced to 9 players (!).
FIFA World Cup 1994 World Cup visits USA! 4th Brazilian samba! 50

FIFA World Cup 1994 Key points Tournament details : •Host country: United States of America (1 st time); •Dates: 17 June – 17 July (31 days); •Teams: 24 (from 5 confederations); •Venue(s): 9 (in 9 host cities). Final positions : •Champions: Brazil (4 th title); 51 Champions: Brazil (4 title); •Runners-up: Italy; •Third place: Sweden; •Fourth place: Bulgaria. Tournament statistics : •Matches played: 52; •Goals scored: 141 (2.71 per match); •Attendance: 3,587,538 (68,991 per match).

FIFA World Cup 1994 Highlights Host selection : •Three bidding nations involved: United States, Brazi l and Morocco; •Only took one round with the United States bid rece iving a little over half of the votes by the executive committee members;•FIFA’s hope; lead to a growth of interest in the sp ort; •Tends to show that financial and sportive aspects a re chained together. First times : •First World Cup in USA; 52 •First World Cup in USA; •First participation: Greece, Nigeria and Saudi Arab ia; •First final involving a penalty shoot-out after a 0 -0 (surprisingly, Italy was involved ☺); •Erwin Sanchez scored the Bolivia's first ever World Cup goal. Notes : •One condition imposed by FIFA was the creation of a professional soccer league; MLS founded in 1993 and operating in 1996; •Defender Andrés Escobar was shot to death outside a b ar in a Medellin suburb; apparently in retaliation for the own goal he score d against the USA; the murderers were never arrested; Colombia a no-law country?

FIFA World Cup 1994 Highlights Results : •The United States' 2–1 victory over Colombia was it s first World Cup victory since 29 June 1950;•Sweden's 3–1 victory over Russia was the nation's 1st WC victory since 3 July 1974; •Oleg Salenko became the 1st (and remains the only) m an ever to score 5 goals in a single WC game (Russia – Cameroon 6-1);•While facing Norway, Italy able to salvage an impor tant 1–0 victory despite the Pagliuca's dismissal… and enters the knock -out round as the last best third; 53 Pagliuca's dismissal… and enters the knock -out round as the last best third; •Italy enters its 5 th final after having defeated Nigeria, Spain and Bulg aria with 2-1 victories (!); •Brazil enters its 5 th final after having defeated USA (1-0), Netherlands (3-2) and Sweden (1-0); •Brazil wins its 4 th crown after a penalty shoot-out (3-2).
FIFA World Cup 1998 Zidane 2, Gronaldo 0 ☺! Cocorico , la première étoile !! 54

FIFA World Cup 1998 Key points Tournament details : •Host country: France (2 nd time); •Dates: 10 June – 12 July (33 days); •Teams: 32 (from 5 confederations); •Venue(s): 10 (in 10 host cities). Final positions : •Champions: France (1 st title); 55 Champions: France (1 title); •Runners-up: Brazil; •Third place: Croatia; •Fourth place: Netherlands. Tournament statistics : •Matches played: 64; •Goals scored: 171 (2.67 per match); •Attendance: 2,785,100 (43,517 per match). Ronaldo a bit disappointed

FIFA World Cup 2006 Highlights Results : •Italy – USA: 1-1 with a 10-against-9-players match; •France enters the knock-out round with a 2-0 victor y over Togo; •Portugal-Netherlands (1-0) was called the ‘ battle of Nuremberg ’ (16 yellow cards and 4 players sent off after a second bookable offense) b ut not comparable with the infamous ‘battle of Santiago ’ in 1962 ☺; •Italy-Australia: 1-0 with a controversial penalty s cored by Totti, but Materazzi got a controversial red card; 64 controversial red card; •France eliminated Spain 3-1 while Spanish supporter s singing ‘ Au revoir Zinedine Zidane ’ (rhythm of Pet Shop Boys’ Go West); •Italy enters its 6 th final by eliminating Ukraine (3-0) and Germany (2-0 aet); •France enters its 2 nd final by eliminating Brazil and Portugal 1-0; •Italy-France: second final involving penalty shoot- out (after Brazil-Italy); •Zidane opened the scoring by converting a controvers ial seventh-minute penalty kick, Materazzi then leveled the scores in the 19th minute following a Pirlo’s corner; •Zidane head-butted Materazzi in the chest in an off-t he-ball incident and was sent off; •Trezeguet (who scored the golden goal against Italy in the Euro 2000 final) didn’t score his penalty (ironic isn’t it ? ☺).

First times : •Both of the finalists from the 2006 WC (Italy and F rance) eliminated in the initial stage of the competition. FIFA World Cup 2014 Host selection : •Held in South America for the first time since 1978 ; •Second consecutive World Cup outside of Europe (1 st time); •Second withdrawal of Colombia from the process. First times : •Bosnia and Herzegovina the only team with no previo us appearance; •Introduction of the goal-line technology to avoid g host goals; •Use of vanishing foam by the referees; Highlights 70 •Use of vanishing foam by the referees; •Colombia and Costa Rica enter the quarter-finals; •Germany is the first European team to win in south America. Results : •Spain became the 4th nation to be eliminated in the first round while holding the World Cup crown; •Argentina enters the final with single-goal margin victories (except for the semi-final, victory over Netherlands after a penalty shoot-out) ; •Germany humiliated Brazil with a 7-1 victory (bigge st defeat in Brazilian history since 1920).

Conclusion Biggest success of sport : •FIFA World Cup now a (the?) world main event; •Widely broadcasted and watched on TV around the wor ld; •2014 World Cup final attracted 1.01 billion viewers (1/6 of humanity!); •No need to talk about the financial aspects ☺. Homogenization of the level : •Results with large margins tend to be rare (althoug h there are always exceptions); •Big fishes may be annoyed or swallowed by the littl e ones (yes, a contradiction ☺); 71 •Big fishes may be annoyed or swallowed by the littl e ones (yes, a contradiction ☺); •Due to dissemination of football techniques around the world and professionalization of football players. But : •UEFA and Conmebol rule over football (11 – 9); •African and Asian teams struggling to enter the sem i finals; •Conmebol decided that Copa America and Euro played th e same year and the winner of Copa will defy the winner of Euro (a sort of Super W orld Cup); •So, shall we use ‘ World Cup ’ or ‘ UEFA-Conmebol Cup ’ (back to 1930 ☺)?
